Funnel Hacking Live, get on your feet and welcome back to the stage the man of the era, Mr. Russell Brunson! What's up everybody? Okay, I know what some of you guys are thinking right now. All right. So I don't think you guys heard or not, but over the last year or so I've been working on a secret project. I'm working on a new book. How many of you guys have heard about this new book? Okay. So here's the story. So I started writing the book, Traffic Secrets almost two years ago now and I do, I always encourage you guys to do it. It's like before you go and create something, it's like you're testing your material, right? So I started going to events. I would talk about traffic. I would do podcasts about it. I would do Facebook lives. I started testing out this material and seeing what things landed, what things didn't land. Alaskan Funnel Hockey Live, I gave a presentation about traffic secrets and shortly after the event ended, I got to work actually starting on the manuscript. I started writing it and writing it. And it's been such a fun project. Also a very intimidating project. Traffic is one of the things that changes the most in the industry. And I was like, how do I write a book that's going to be evergreen for forever without talking about things that are going to be obsolete by the time this thing goes to print? And so that was a big undertaking. It took me almost a full year to get the book done. And when the book got done, we got our publisher and they said, hey, do you want to do this as a hardbound book? And I was like, yes, that would be insanely cool to have a hardbound book. And I was like, wait, if we have that book hardbound, I want all the books hardbound. And so we had a different publisher for the first two books to the paperback editions. And so I went back and I bought the rights back from them. And I said, and I asked the new publisher, Hey House, I said, hey, will you guys be willing to republish these books? All his hardbounds. And they're like, yeah, that'd be cool. I was like, then we could do a box set. It would be like Harry Potter. It'd be amazing. I was like, freak it out. It's like, oh, yeah, you can do that. And so we're getting down to traffic secrets. And then I sent the manuscript to them and all the artwork and stuff. And they said, now it's done. And I was like, well, I have a question. Now that I have a little window, can I edit a couple of chapters and add a couple of stories and stuff? And just before I give it to you, they're like, oh, sure, yeah, just don't do too much. I'm like, OK, no worries. So I got the whole manuscript and I started reading first the dotcom secrets book. I started reading it. And I was like two chapters in. I was like, oh my gosh, I sucked at writing books five years ago. I was like, I didn't tell stories very well. I was so frustrated. I was like, oh my gosh. There's a lesson behind this. The lesson is that Russell five years ago sucked at writing books. But guess what Russell five years ago still did? Wrote a book. So I'm going through the book and I'm like, this is not as good as it should be. And I'm going to make my little edits, but then I'm going to see how deep it goes. And so the book started out at 58,000 words. I ended up deleting over 30,000 words and ended up at 94,000 words when all was done. So that's the new dotcom secrets book. And then I slipped into the publisher. I'm like, here's the book with a couple of edits. She's like, how much of it is edited? I was like, all the pages with words on it are new. Sorry. She's killing it and she's following the same kind of process. And so I'm going to walk you through that as well. So that way, as you step into any platform, you know, the process and the framework to do to be able to be successful in there. That's only fun. Okay. So this is the, the first framework we're going to is the dream 100 for a lot of you guys, this is going to be review. I'm going to go over it kind of quickly. I'm going to spend more time on the sec second half, but it's such a core fundamental piece of the entire picture on to make sure I spend a little time on it right now. Okay. So the first thing is you are figuring out how to drive traffic. It all comes down to this. Who is your dream customer? Okay. And this is like such an essential piece of it. And I wish that somebody, when I first started my business, would grab me by the ears and said, Russell, stop trying to create new products. Try to figure out who your dream customer is first. Okay. Cause I was six years into my business and I, I was laying in bed, I woke up on morning and I realized I hated my company. I didn't hate the people that worked for me, but I hated the people we were serving. How many guys kind of don't love your customers right now? Couple of you guys are willing to admit it. Okay. I woke up that day. I was just like, I'm miserable. I was like, I remember laying there in bed saying, I wish I had a boss so this person could fire me cause I do not want to go to work that day. It's because I was creating products and customers were coming to me and I had this realization. Like I need to first figure out who is my dream customer and then create the products that will actually attract them. Okay. And so I spent some time doing an exercise to figure out who are my dream customers. And I won't go too deep into that, but the basic gist is I really keep being clear on that. Who do I actually want to serve? What do they look like? Where do they, how do they act? Where do they, what are they doing? And I got super crystal clear on exactly who my dream customers were. Okay. And this exercise I did about, about six years ago. Okay. How many of you guys have been drawn into our community and our world in the last six years? Every single person who I've brought to this event is their first time is like, this audience is different. These people are crazy. Like, this is not like a normal business event. This is like a rock concert. Right? Okay. That was strategic by design. I was going to marketing events and I was like, Lord have on my sitting in back sleeping. Like, how was everybody so bored? Like, I want to attract the kind of people who are going to be passionate about this. We're going to wake up at five in the morning and line up outside because they're so excited to learn some more stuff. People aren't sitting in the halls talking. They want to be in the room because they know that the best stuff's happening right here. Like those are the people I want to attract. Right? And so it started there. Figuring that out. So for you guys, you have to figure out exactly who it is you want to serve. That's the first step in business, not the last. Okay. The second step after you know who they are, then you got to figure out where in the world are those people actually congregating. Okay. The magic of the internet and why we're going to have success and build companies so fast is because people online, they already self congregate together based on similar beliefs and values and things that they love. Think about in your personal life, the things that you're super passionate about. Right? We all have those things. Okay. So for me, the things I passed on, I'm obviously passionate about marketing and sales and business. I'm passionate about entrepreneurship, personal development. I'm passionate about biohacking. I'm passionate about supplements. I'm passionate about wrestling. I'm passionate about the LDS church. I'm passionate about a lot of different things. Right? These things that I love and I care about. And if I think about my life, if I step back and like, where do I spend my time on the internet? I'm usually tapping into some of these communities. Right? I'm going to hang out with the marketers over here. I'm going to hang out with the wrestlers over here. I'm going to hang out and I'm hanging out in different spots based on the things that are interesting to me. Right? And all of you guys do the exact same thing. So start thinking like, where are the places I'm already congregating based on the things I'm excited about? Where I'm finding my tribe and my people. Right? Okay. And so that's the question that is out. I know where I'm going. Like, where are my people? Like my dream customers, where are they already congregating? If I can find those people and I can throw my hooks out in front of them, I can start getting them. Okay. And so that's the second thing. Who's my dream customer and then where are they hiding? Where are they congregating? And then you just got to go find those people. Okay. I've shared this, this example a ton of times with one of the most simple ones. Um, then the time that I really got it when, was when I was in, uh, I was a Boise State with my junior year in college and I was just learning this whole marketing thing. And I remember traffic to me seemed like this magic trick. Right? I kept trying to figure how do I create traffic? How do I create traffic? And I remember I had a mentor at the time, his name is Steven Pierce and Steven Pierce said, no, no, you don't understand Russell. You don't try to create traffic. Traffic's already there. He's used it to find the existing traffic streams that are already happening and then just step in front of them and the traffic will come to you. I was like, I don't have to create traffic. No, no, no, just find it. Don't create it. Go and find it. And I'm sitting there in study hall, my junior year, I was wrestling and all the wrestlers struggled at school. Most of us, um, were on the, the list every single week, saying like grade checks and things like that. So we had mandatory study hall, two hours every single night for all wrestlers. Um, and so every, every night after practice, we'd go and then we'd study off for two hours and we're supposed to be doing our homework. And I remember looking around as I was doing my homework and all the other computers, all the other wrestlers were all on the exact same website. We were all on the mat.com and all this were there. And we were in the forums, we were talking about wrestling and we were talking about who's, who's good wrestling, who's speaking in the matches. And we're talking about, uh, just all the stuff about wrestling, right? We're geeking out. And I was looking at, I was looking around. I realized that of the 30 or so wrestlers in the room, probably 18 of us were on the mat right now, not doing our homework, talking. And then I jumped in the form. I started looking at it. I was like, there are hundreds and hundreds of people live right now, all talking about these same topics. I was like, there's probably people in every single, um, wrestling, uh, community across the country, every single college has wrestling, every single high school, all around the world. They're all coming together on our little corner of the internet where all those wrestlers can hang out and have some fun. And I was thinking, I was like, if I had a wrestling product, just so where would I go? How would I create traffic? I don't need to create traffic. I just find the existing traffic is all right there. All of them, they're hanging out. I'm just coming here and put my hooks out in front of it. Okay. So that's why this traffic game becomes easy when you start looking at it differently. It's just all you gotta do is figure out who's my dream customer, where are they at and then how do I throw my hooks in front of them? Okay. That's really traffic when it's all said and done. There's little tactics about ways to be better and worse, but for the most part, that's the game. Okay. It really, really is easy. Okay. So let me jump in the framework now. So the first step in the framework, now we understand who's our dream customer and where they congregating. The first question I start asking is who has already congregated my dream customers? Okay. Who are those people? What are the places? What are the, what are the blogs they read? What are the email lists are on? What are the forms that are part of what Facebook groups are they are they on? Who, what influencers they follow? Like where, where are all the different places they're already currently congregating? And so we build the list. And I like building a, back in the day, I didn't understand how different each platform was. So I said a huge list of like everybody, every single person's thing. Uh, over the last, probably three and a half, four years, we've gotten very good at breaking down and say, okay, who were all the dream 100 on Facebook? Who's already congregated my dream people on Facebook, on Instagram, which podcast they listened to and we break it down by every single platform. Okay. How many of you guys have, have already built out your dream 100 list so far? Okay. So maybe 10% of you guys, I literally talked about dream 100, every presentation for the last 15 years of my life. So that means for everybody else who's out of pen and tension, this is step one. Okay. When we were launching click funnels, um, the two weeks before click funnels launched, uh, Todd and Dylan flew to Boise and we sat up all night and they were coding, um, they were coding click funnels, right? Um, and they were set up all night drinking red bulls, doing all this stuff, like coding. And I'm me who on the non-technical co-founder, I have no technical abilities whatsoever. Um, I'm sitting there and I want to support them. I'm hanging out and I'm like, what do I do? What do I do? I gotta do something. So guess what I did for two weeks, all nighters for two weeks straight while they were coding, trying to get click funnels live. I was doing this at a list. All right. Who else on Facebook? Who else on Instagram? I started to make this list and I was calling him. I was contacting, getting to know them, sending him packages in the mail. Like while they were coding, I was doing this. Okay. That's how much value this is. Like this is the most important thing you could be doing. If you want to get attention into your business is figuring out this. Who's already got your dream customers. Okay. So that's step number one. We got our dream customers. After we know who they are, then we can go through two different processes. Number one, we're going to try to work our way into those groups. And number two, we're going to try to buy our way in and break those. I'll break those down next. Okay. So step number two, now you know our dream 100 is now we got to work our way in. It was interesting about this as we see this every single day in the real world. Yeah. For some reason, we dismiss and we forget about it online. Think about every time you guys watch late night talk shows. Let me just watch early morning shows. Okay. If you notice every time there's a new movie comes out, guess what they do? They take the actor and the actresses who are the big names or whatever the show is. And then these people start going to the big shows, right? They go on Ellen, they go on today. So they go on the morning show. They go on Jimmy Kim or Jimmy Fallon and they're going out there. And they're promoting their business. They're working their way in, right? They're talking about this week and the show's coming up. They tell you about the show and they talk all about it. They show a quick clip from it. And like this weekend, go and watch the show. It's going to be amazing. Okay. All Hollywood's doing for the week leading up to the premiere is they're working their way and they're sending out their, their, their attractive characters. They're putting one on the road. They're making it from interview to interview to interview to interview. They're on TV, they're on radio, they're on podcasts, on everything. All promoting shows about to launch. Okay. If Hollywood does that's the same way we should be doing it. So you got a funnel. It's going live or it is live. This should be our jobs. And we earn this traffic. Okay. Let's go to our dream 100 and figure out how we can, how we can work our way in. Okay. If you listened to, to Julie's Dean yesterday, when you talked to how he got his videos viral, how did he do it? He made some content, contact the people to see if they share, made some content. Boom. He was working his way in, working his way in. And now he's six million followers. Okay. Like that's the magic of working your way in. So I go back to my dream 100 list and I started looking at all these people and we're not going to be able to work our way into all of them, but what people are there? That I can start working my way into. Okay. And sometimes one of the mistakes people make. I was so grateful. Julie's talked about this yesterday too. Is they come in like, okay, I'm going to get Tony Robbins to be my dream 100. That's my only one dream. 100. If I can get him, I'll get everybody else. They start going after Tony, start going after Tony or whoever after Russell after whoever the big, the big dog is. And the problem with that is that the big dog is not going to pay attention to you at first. Okay. But Julie says that I found people on my same level. Right. And I did collabs with them and then their, their fan page grew, mine grew together and then they're bigger and I could go after bigger people. And then we shared, we shared, we got bigger and got bigger. And eventually now Julius is the man. He literally shared a little thing on stage was like, I'm on stage at Russell Brunson. I had like, I don't know, two or 3000 people follow me yesterday from his Instagram because he's like, Hey, I'm here with Russell. Like crazy. Thank you, Julius. By the way, that was amazing. So you should tag me more often. Um, in fact, all the guys should tag me more often. I appreciate it. Anyway, so we're working our way in, right? So we're figuring out, okay, how do we work our way in? And that's in every platform is different. Okay. You can go, someone has got a huge blog. Go to him and say, Hey, can I write a guest blog post? Let me do a guest blog post. If someone's got a big podcast. So, Hey, I've got a really cool show. I did. You can want to interview me on your show, uh, video, collab, shout outs. There's all these different ways to go into your dream. 100 and figure out how do we work together where I can work my way in to get access to your audience? Okay. This big stream of traffic started here. How do I work my way in so I can step into the stream of traffic and get a piece of it as well? Okay. The first 10 years of my business, I didn't pay a single dollar in ads. 100% of my, my business came from this working my way in. Okay. I've a lot of people like, I want to do a funnel by camp because I have no money for Facebook ads. Neither did I for 10 years. 10 years is all I do is I work my way in, work my way in, work my way in. Okay. Same thing for you. Like you don't have to have money to get to, to launch a traffic campaign. You just have to have time and some of us have more time than money. It's something to have more money than time. So depending where you start is going to be based on which one of those things you have, you have more money than time, but like focus first on buying your way in. If you have more time than money, then focus your, your, your efforts on working your way to start with. Okay. All right. So now we got traffic. We're earning our way. Now we're shifting to traffic that we control. This is where we buy our way in. Okay. And this is simply going to our dream 100, right? And buying paid ads to their followers and we're throwing hooks out in front of them. Okay. So I know that Troy Rawls and I dream 100. He's not going to let me do a podcast interview with him, but I can target his followers on Facebook or Instagram. I can throw my hooks in. I can buy my way in. I can start pulling people out of his, his, uh, his pond of woman to mine as well. Okay. And step number four, the whole goal of all of traffic building is to get people onto your own list. That's the big secret. Okay. Um, the reason why click phones has grown is we're doing a whole bunch of stuff to, to, uh, who buy ads everywhere. But if you notice all of our ads are focused on something to get them to give us their email address. So now they're on email list. Okay. Most of this room was not filled by paid ads. Most of this room was filled by people who would clicked on a paid ad, came to something opted in. We were able to send emails for last year or two years, five years from you guys. And the emails were what pulled you in. Right. The email list is the biggest value in your company. That's when you own your own traffic. And that's the key. So if you look at this dream 100, this framework as a whole are our goals to figure out who our dream customers are, where they congregating, who are the dream 100 already have them. And then we work our way in and we buy our way in. And the goal from both of those is to get people to join our list. Okay. So if I'm buying ads, I'm saying, Hey, here's a cool ad. Here's my free framework. Come opt in to get a free framework. They come from the, from the traffic I bought. They give me the email address. Now it's traffic that I own. Okay. If I'm on a podcast interview, I do the interview with the person. I try to help as much as possible at the end of it. I'm like, Hey, um, if you guys want to free copy my framework, go to blah, blah, blah. Dot com and get my free thing. Right. And so I'm just pushing people from both the both sides of these things into a spot where they can join my email list. Okay. I'm not sure he has an example about here in a second, but that's, that's the goal. Okay. So basically section one of this book is, is that, um, oh, actually, I do have a really cool case to share with you guys right now. I listened to a lot of podcasts. My two favorite podcasts are mixer G and business wars. If you guys aren't listening to those, they're amazing. Mixer G is Andrew Warner and he interviews a startup founder or two or three times a week about their whole startup story, which is really, really fun. Just to hear just tons of people's startup stories. Uh, and then, um, business wars, who here's ever heard of business wars? Oh, for the rest of you guys, I just changed your life. Okay. So business wars is, uh, it's like an actual show. So that's basically the first section here, the traffic secrets book. It's the foundation of everything. Cause all the other things I teach, like they, they come off of that foundation. So I want to make sure that, that we shared that with you guys first. Okay. So now the second section of the book is called the fill your funnel frameworks. Okay. And the second section, again, this is the part of the book that was really, um, not scary, but like the hardest part for me to write because traffic's changes so often, right? It's always changing. I'm like, how in the world are right and never green book about the most un-evergreen topic in the history of the planet? And I was like, the only way to do it is to figure out what's the framework, what's the pattern? What is that thing that works every single time? I started looking back like, okay, back before there was a Facebook, how did I get traffic? Okay. How many of you guys remember before, uh, Facebook, there was a site called my space, who remembers my space? Okay. Who remembers before my space, there was a site called Friendster. Yeah. Less, right? Okay. And so I started looking like, like, how did we get traffic back then? I started looking at what we did and I started looking at the pattern. I started looking at the new things. And I was like, Oh my gosh, there's a pattern that I do every single time consistently. And, um, it's funny because Rachel Pearson can get me excited about TikTok. I keep trying to find it, but every time she posts something and then my daughter Ellie is obsessed. And so we make TikTok videos together. And, um, so I'm like, okay, I'm going to start playing this game again. And so as I started looking at TikTok, I started looking into the same framework and started getting, started getting really, really exciting. So I'm going to teach you guys this framework and you can use it in any platform. I don't care if it's Google, YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, Pinterest, new, new platform coming out in the future. If you understand this framework, um, you can go into any of these networks and you can have success. Okay. So step number one, the first thing you have to understand when you go into any new platforms, if you're going to go to Google or you're going to go to YouTube or whatever it is, first you have to understand, you have to understand the history of where this platform has come and the goal of the founders of the platform. Okay. You have to understand where it came from so you can know where it's going. These things are shifting and changing all the time. Okay. So you don't understand where it came from. It's hard to know where it's going. And then you're just kind of hitting, missing, hoping for the best, right? So for example, how many guys know who these two people are? Is that crazy? Most of you guys don't know. So these are the founders of Google. This is Larry and Sergey. They founded Google. And when they founded Google initially, it was kind of crazy. It wasn't called Google. They would know that it was called initially when they first created it. It's called back rub. That's kind of weird, huh? Let me tell you why. So they were sitting there and they were trying to figure out this whole search thing. The way search engines work prior to Google is that basically people had search engine results that they would list stuff. But then if you want to be ranked high, it was 100% like you pay 500 bucks a year and you'll be listed in the search engine. Right. And that's how most of search engines work. So you were getting, if you go search for something, all you ever saw like basically paid ads, like that's all the search engines work. And so whoever rose the top was whoever was willing to pay the most. And they're like, I don't feel like that's the right thing. Like, and so their philosophy, their hypothesis going into this is like, I bet you that whoever gets the most other sites saying that they're cool, like that's just like whoever's linking the most back to this one site. That's how we know if someone's the best, right? And so they built their first algorithm that's all based on that. So they basically came back and said, okay. Here's their search is ultra. Here's number one, number two, number three, four, number five, all the way down 10, right? So here's a website here. Each one's a website. And it's okay. If a whole bunch of people are linking to this, let's say this site's got, got, let's just, okay. People linking here, people linking. Let's say this site's got a hundred links. If this one has a hundred and one means more people like this site. This one has a hundred and two links. It means more people like this site. So it's like, that means they're rubbing each other's back, right? Like, like this is like, they have a lot of people who like this person. And so that was kind of their, their philosophy. So they created out the initial search engine. They put it out there and the ranking shifted and it worked really, really good. At the time, I think they were, I can't remember their Stanford, Harvard blanked off top of my head, but they wanted schools. This is where it was built out initially. And eventually they were using the school servers. It got too big. They couldn't handle it. And so they said, you have to take it off of this platform and go take it somewhere else. They bought Google.com and it became, it became Google. Okay. So this is, this is the whole history of the platform, how it worked was like, okay. We got to figure out who's got the most links wins. Okay. And then what happens as soon as the platform goes live and the algorithm is out there, then people like us, we're the entrepreneurs who were like, this is really, really cool. How do we beat the algorithm? How do we beat the system? What's the loophole? Right. How many of us look at everything through that lens sometimes? Okay. We ruin the internet, just so you guys know. And I was part of the first wave of ruining the internet. So I remember sitting back and hearing this and people like this out works. I remember like some of these keywords, especially if you get on the first page like a really competitive keyword, it can be worth hundreds of thousands or millions of dollars to you. Right. And so it became this really quick game. We said, okay, well, um, I want to be in this spot. How many links that they have 102? Cool. I'm going to go and get a thousand links. And we started hiring people in Philippines and in India and Peru to start doing link building for us. Right. I said, this site's got a hundred links. We want 5,000 and boom, we blow it up, 5,000 links. And instead we were number one, right? We're number one in Google. Right. And then we started creating software that would make mass spamming links as our spamming is we get higher and higher and higher. And so for a little while, all the spammers and the scammers, the ones who rose to the top, right? Which then makes Google's algorithm no good because now it's like, oh my gosh, we're supposed to show the best sites. Now we're showing literally the worst sites from people who are just spamming the search engines. So they had to go back to work. Like we have to shift the algorithm. How do we change it? And so they start shifting this to, okay, well, maybe it's not just links, maybe it's quality links, maybe, and then let's give paid drink to things. And they've gone through a whole bunch of iterations over the years to get this closer and closer and closer to something that works. Okay. And so there's a season with a new network where people are trying to, to game the system figured out while the, while the founders are trying to figure out how to, how to, how to protect themselves. And eventually, you know, 15, 20 years later, now we got Google words now, and it's much more difficult to break the search engines. So instead we say, instead of trying to break the search engines, what if we align with the search engines? What if you get what they actually want? Right? This is the mindset shift I had to have from going from like me being a hacker to try to figure out how to just game the system. Like how do we play with the system? Okay. Cause I've had a lot of times I got pays number one for make money, pays number one for work at home pays them for all these keywords for a season where for two or three weeks I'm getting insane amounts of leads and all of a sudden it's gone. Okay. So in six months, getting something there and then week that was there for a week and it's gone and it's like a painful, not fun game. I was like, I want to build stuff that's going to be consistent. It's going to last long-term. So to do that, I got to figure out where is Google going? Where is YouTube going? Where's Twitter, Pinterest, whatever the platform, where is it going? What are their goals? Like an align with them? Okay. It's the mindset shift for us entrepreneurs cause we're always trying to think how do we break the rules instead of when you start shifting to how do we align with their rules? Cause if we can do that, we can build something that'll be stable and long-term. Right? So that's the number one is figuring out the platform, understanding where they came from, where they're going. So we can align with their goals and figure out the stuff they actually want. Okay. That way you can be doing this for a long time. That's number one. Step number two now, this is where we go to that platform. So I don't care what everyone it is, you go to the platform that you want to be on. So if you pick Instagram or Facebook, you pick the platform, you go there and you start finding your dream 100, right? You want to figure out who were the dream 100 now on this specific platform. Okay. Somebody's already there. They've already congregated your people. So let's find out what those people are. Okay. Now I want to recommend all you guys do the first thing I want you to do when you go to new platform, let's say it's Instagram, go to Instagram. The first thing you need to do is you need to unsubscribe from all of your friends and your family. All they are doing in your life is bringing you stress and headaches and conversations that are useless for you growing business. These phones are not social tools. This social media is not a social tool for you guys. Okay. We are producers of social media. We are not consumers of social media. All right. Man, you can run around a rabbit hole and lose your life very, very quickly. So what I recommend is unsubscribe for everyone who's not on your dream 100. Okay. This becomes a market research show really quickly. You stop following every single person that you knew in high school. Okay. We unsubscribe from them all and then we just follow our dream 100. Okay. I start following on my dream 100 and then every single day I put my phone, I scroll through this thing. I'm just doing market research. What are my dream 100 doing right now? How are they speaking to my audience? What are they saying? What are they being rewarded for? What things are showing up my feet and what things aren't. Okay. So we have to be looking constantly every single day. Like what's, what are they being rewarded for today? If you got what things are working, we're looking at every single book, like all the other people are dream 100 to look what they're doing and figure out what's the pattern, what's working today. I remember probably four or five years ago. Um, man, maybe three, I don't know, a couple of years ago, um, uh, uh, looking through Instagram and I, I'm kind of weird because I follow my dream 100, but I follow the dream 100 and like almost every industry is cause I'm obsessed with like seeing what people are doing. And so, um, I follow the fitness people, all the real estate people, like all the, like I'm following all the people so I can see what's happening in other markets as well. I remember, um, seeing in my feet, all the fitness people started using these meme videos. So you guys remember, uh, you guys know what meme videos are? Was like a video with the headline up top and then transcripts down below. I remember every time I sit, I was like, with cats, my cats means I'm scrolling to them like, Oh, that thing is cool. And I look at it in the comments and the likes were super high. I'm like, what in the world is this thing? And I started looking at it. I remember like a month or so later, we had an inner circle meeting and Brandon and Kailin came up and when they did their presentation, they're like, you guys, meme videos are killing it for us. They showed us right. Oh my gosh. And so all of a sudden, all of us in the inner circle meeting that day went out, started to do meme videos on our own platforms. And for a while, it was the greatest thing since sliced bread. Okay. What you have to understand is that social media marketing is all about patterns and pattern interrupts, right? So what happens is that everybody starts looking the same, starts going through your feet and everything looks the same. And also something interrupts you, right? As you create these pattern interrupts. So for a little while, those meme videos were the pattern interrupt. You're going through it. It's like, Oh my gosh, we stopped it, right? It stopped your thing. And then other people started singing. They model, they copy it. And eventually the pattern interrupt becomes the pattern. Right. And that happens quickly. Sometimes you got a month, some of two months, three months before the pattern interrupt becomes pattern and stopped working is good. So we're always looking at what's working. What's the new pattern interrupt? What can we try next? What's the next thing we can do? What's the, what's the next pattern interrupt? We can figure out, we can get people to stop in their tracks as they're scrolling through, right? So that's why I'm doing this. I'm following my dream 100. I'm scrolling through here, looking what they're doing to figure out what do things to get me to stop? What do things get me to click? Right. So I can look at that and I start looking at the post like, what did they do? Like, was it a short comment? Was the log comment? Okay. Cause someone will ask me, what else, what should my comments look like? What should my posts look like on Instagram? I'm like, I don't know. It changes day by day. Like what are the algorithms rewarding today? The way you find that out is figure out from your dream 100, who are the people having success in watching them and modeling them and be like, Oh my gosh. I just like, for example, I saw Grant Cardone yesterday posted this really cool sales video on things, Instagram. We had $100 bills. Do you even see this? And he had like his sales video written on $100 bills and magic markers. He's flipping thing after thing after thing. I watched the whole entire thing. I am so busy. I'm planning a higher event for 5,000 people. And I'm sitting there watching this video. Like it was such a good pattern to interrupt. And you can bet everything you got. I'm trying to figure out how to, like, what do I create? Like I'm not going to $100 bills because that's ridiculous, but like, could I do something like $1 bills? Like how can I like model that? Right. And then eventually every single one of you guys are in the same thing and it becomes the patterns. Like, Oh good. Another dollar bill like thing. Right. But further, a little window there, there's a window. So it's looking for the patterns and it's always trying to develop your own thing too. Like do something crazy. Do something weird. Okay. There's a big reason why Bart has been wearing two to three outfits every single day, right? Because I don't want you guys to go through your social media and see Russell in the same clothes 800 times. I want you to be like, Oh my gosh, there's a picture of Russell with this thing. Like what is that? I stopped you in your tracks. I Russell in a suit. Like that's weird. It's a pattern interrupt. Right. That's why we do anyway. So that's what we're looking for. So we're looking for these pattern interrupts. Um, now, uh, Jenna, this is Jenna. Could you guys know Jenna? If you guys don't, she is one of my favorite human beings on this planet. Um, we were hoping to get her to speak at this funnel, hack you live, and she was at their family in Hawaii. Um, but I'm going to be hanging out there next week and maybe we can get her next year. If all you guys comment our stuff, say we wish you were at funnel hacking live. I bet we can get her to come here next year. So yeah, you already did. So anyway, not that I'm trying to get her here. She's amazing. So, um, but I was, uh, she's been on my dream 100 for a long time. Now she's a really good podcast called the gold digger podcast. You should all listen to it. Um, but I was scrolling through, uh, the feed and I keep seeing her stuff. And, um, and I remember, uh, just being really impressed and looking at all her, all her posts, so many comments and gauge them. I'm like, she knows what she's doing. And so I went and, uh, and then I said, okay, she knows what she's doing. I went and bought her courses. I started following her, started studying her. I got on the phone, got to know her and start asking her questions. I was like, man, you were one of the most engaging people on social media. I've ever seen. How do you do it? And she said, well, I have a framework I use. I'm like, what's your framework? She said, my framework is called the JK five method. You guys heard of Jenna's JK five method before? It's a simple framework. She created for how she posts on Instagram. And ever since she taught me this, we started doing it now on Facebook and like podcasts. I try to model this everywhere, but basically the JK five method is you got to find five different categories of your life that people will find interesting. Okay. What happens for a lot of us is like, we're business people or we're fitness people. So everything we post is like, is me doing a squat, right? Or it's me like on stage going like this, right? What happens is you're very one dimensional that way. Right. And so the people who are like pumped, they want to be on stage will follow you, but the rest of the world is tunes you out. She said, you have to be a multi-dimensional human because we all are anyway. We said, you have to be multi-dimensional. She says, you have to pick five categories of things that you are passionate about. So for me, I was like, okay, what are my five categories? And these are the five I picked. I said, families, funnels, faith, entrepreneurship and personal development. These are the five things that I'm really passionate about. She said, next on your phone, you need to make five album, picture albums in your phone for these five different categories. And then she says throughout the day, you'll be taking pictures of every single thing you're doing and then categorizing them. One of these five things. So if you're taking your kids to church, take a picture, if you're going to an event, take a picture, if you're going, like everywhere you're going to start taking pictures of all these different things and put them in the categories in your album on your phone. So you have them. And then what you do is when you start posting in social media, you don't do two business pictures in a row. You rotate through your five categories, your JK5. So first you post a picture of your family, then one with funnels, then faith and entrepreneurship and personal development. Okay. So if you look at mine right here, so the top left-hand corner, there's me and my wife and our amazing kids in McCall, Idaho, going skiing, right? So there's a family one. Then you go to the right, there's me droodling out of funnel. Boom, there's a funnel one, right? Then you go to the right, there's me and Fiji with a preacher preaching to the Fijian or, uh, yeah, preaching with an Epentecostal church with this dude, which was amazing, right? There's faith. Then we go to entrepreneurship. There's my kids selling lemonade stand. We go to personal development. There's talking about repetition matters. Let me start back over. There's family, right? You start seeing this pattern. They're not a hundred percent. Sometimes we kind of, you know, not a hundred percent perfect, but there's a pattern to this, right? So what happens is, is you start doing this and you'll have people from different parts of your life that will follow you, right? People that go to church with me and start following me and they're like, oh, there's Russell and his family. We got to meet him. And then he starts seeing pictures of my family. Oh, this is cool. I like Russell. And then like, oh, what's that weird personal development thing? What's this funnel thing? What's this? And all of a sudden they start seeing this and eventually they start asking questions that are coming into your world. And they're like, oh, I like Russell because I know he's a family person. I know his family. I trust him there. What's he doing over here? Okay. I got in this audience, probably five or six people that go to church with me who didn't know what I was when they follow me on Instagram. And now they're here at funnel hacking live, right? Okay. That wasn't by design. That's what happened, right? Same thing with personal development. Entrepreneurs, family, like you do these things out. Like my family posts, right? Like I go to my kids soccer game and no one knows what I do. They all assume I'm unemployed or something that he drives. This weird Jeep says funnel hacker, but they get, I get to know him at soccer games and stuff. They follow us on Instagram or on Facebook and they see me post a picture of my family over and over and over again. Right? We've got the guy who's done our kitchen remodel is here in the audience. It's crazy. He came to our house to know anything what we're doing. You know, it's like I'm building this kitchen at Russell's house. It's kind of weird. Follow me on Instagram. So listen to our podcast and now he's a funnel hacking live, right? And Todd, you having a good time so far? Anyway, but that's what we're doing, right? We're being multifaceted people because people are coming to your world from a whole bunch of different places. A lot of you guys didn't come here because you want to learn funnels. Mostly I didn't know what funnels were, but you saw an entrepreneurial quote, right? We saw a personal development quote. We saw me with Tony Robbins. You're like, Oh, I know Tony Robbins. What's this funnel thing? And all of a sudden, boom, you're drinking the Kool-Aid and now you were here. Okay. The Kool-Aid's good, right? Okay. Yes. Okay. So that's what we're doing is we're doing those things and we're trying to create. So we're taking pictures, you're doing stuff, your documentary things, putting them in folders, then what's time to post that, you just go to your family album or whatever your thing is, your fitness album, scroll through, find the best picture and you post it, right? And that's when we start creating these content and try to figure out these new pattern interrupts and things we can do to grab people's attention. Okay. Like I said, I try to do this now through all my, every year I'm publishing, I'm doing some variation of this, right? From Facebook to Instagram to podcasts. I'm trying to rotate through things so I can be more and more thought of dimensional. I can, I can attract different people into our world. Okay. Phase number three. Now we've got to create our publishing strategy. Okay. We found our dream 100, we're following them. We're seeing what they're doing. Now we got to look at this and say, what's my publishing strategy? Okay. It's going to be different for everybody. Um, and it's going to shift throughout time. Like when we first started doing Instagram a little while ago, we were looking at what are the best people, people have the most success, what are they doing? They were posting, I think twice a day at the time. So I called the twice a day and as we kept doing that, over time we started seeing a drop in engagement, right? What, what's happening? We started looking at people who show up in success and they were posting less often. So she said, okay, we're going to shift from two times a to one time a day. Right. With our podcast for a while, I was doing them random, like all those two podcasts whenever I want to. And eventually I was like, well, what, what, what days we get the biggest, uh, downloads on our podcast? What, what are the things happening? Like let's, let's focus and now I'm going to do it twice a week. Right now in twice a week, they go out at the same time, same day. Cause we figure out what works best for us. Right. So however you're, whatever platform you're going on, you got to figure out, uh, a publishing strategy you can be consistent with. Okay. Consistency is the key. Cause most of the time you go into a platform posting once or twice is not going to do me very good. Okay. It's posting once twice for a year and then two years and then five years. Right. I think I am seven years into my podcast now, but it's consistent. I remember when I started my podcast, I was like, if I have to go into a studio with a microphone and a thing and people editing audios, I will never do this podcast. Cause I can't be consistent with it. But I said, but if I start a podcast called marketing my car and I just drive around and I talk on my phone, I, I drive my car almost every day. Like I'll just do it that way. And so that's how I started jumped from my car. I started driving. I started telling my story, started documenting my journey. Okay. That was pre-click funnels. That was, uh, on the backend of a huge business failure. And we started, started publishing, started publishing. Right. And so that's the key is just being consistent, figuring out a way you can be consistent with. So don't go, um, do TikTok. If you know, there's no way I'm going to be posting videos twice a day of me dancing and lip syncing. Okay. Don't do it. Okay. But if you're like, this is, this fits my vibe as well. I like to do, I enjoy this and then do that. Right. Pick one platform you can be consistent on. Okay. If you're a writer, you're like blogging, like go to medium.com seven free account. Start blogging on medium. Right. If you're a podcast person, do a podcast, like figure out the platform that you want to go into where you can publish consistently and then start doing that. Okay. You create a publishing strategy and then you stick it to it. Okay. Now you have your publishing strategy that says podcasting, your podcast. And now it's like, okay, what are the other podcasts my people are listening to? Okay. I don't have to go and create traffic. I got to find the existing traffic and step in front of it. Okay. All right. It was your working your way in. One thing that I learned the hard way. So I am obviously a hardcore direct response marketer who loves to sell stuff. How many guys are cut from the same thread as me? Okay. Um, so I remember social media first came out. And I bought a Twitter bot that like, you should have like a friend of million people and an unfriend of math. And I'm everything's so mad and just like perplexed. Like, I don't get it. I got a following. I'm sending offers in front of them and why are they not giving me money? Um, and I remember sitting there being like, this is, this is amazing. And so after the dust settled, like all the period, I was like, dude, I don't, I don't get it. Um, how, how did you just do like, I've been spamming people on Twitter for months and nobody's about anything. Like, how do you do this? And he's like, he smiles. He's like, Russell, he's like, I know exactly why I haven't success. Michael, why? Like explain this to me. He's like, um, you know, when you go to like a networking party, I was like, no, I'm introvert. I'm scared of people. He's like, you didn't have been a networking party. I'm like, no, he's like, well, okay. Well, normal people, they go to networking party. He's like, if you go to a party and you walk into the room, right? There's a whole bunch of people hanging out, having fun. You walk in, you're like, Hey guys, what's up? Want to buy my stuff? Cause they guess what they do. Everyone's like, Oh, it's that guy. And they turn their back like, Oh, right. He's like, that's what you did. You're that guy. It's like, I'm that guy. He's like, yeah, you walked into this party and start telling me about your stuff. He's like, nobody likes that guy at the party. Nobody likes you, Russell. I was like, Oh, that sucks. I was like, well, how do you play this game then? He said, okay, this is how as extraverts do it. He's like, you go in the party and you become the life of the party. You talk to people, you have fun, you tell stories, you have to tell jokes. You're going to reduce people into each other. You're like the person who's like the connector and you have a bunch of fun. And suddenly everyone's like, man, Perry is the, is the, is the life of the party. He's the most fun thing. Right? He's like, you're having fun this party. And then he said, when the party's over, you invite people to come back to your house. So they come to your house, they walk in the house and they see the picture of your family on the thing. They see the things are important. You see your bookshelf, see all the stuff that you care about is like there in that situation, then at your house, where it's you and them, different, different location away from the party. That's where you invite them to buy stuff. I was like, Oh my gosh. You said, look at social media as a party. You're going to these places, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, YouTube, LinkedIn, all these places. You're not going to spam. So if you're going to go build a relationship to be the life of the party, you get to know people, get to connect people with each other, become the person that everybody loves. Again, you're coming in, you're fighting, you know, Gary V's got this big following that Tony's not just following your fight, our dream 100, how big are the following and then we start throwing in hooks, right? Again, the traffic is already there. We're not trying to, we're not trying to generate it or create it. We're just finding, boom, there's traffic. There's 3.1 million people following Tony. Let me just see how I can step into that and get some people to come to me as well. Okay. And then last phase here in the last 10 seconds is filling your funnel. Okay. If you have nothing else in this business, the key to business is growing your list. Okay. Um, traffic is going to go up and it's going to go down. It's going to shift. It's going to be really good. Sometimes it'll be really, really bad sometimes. Kevin, this is, I think this is my 16th year in this industry. I have seen thousands and thousands of entrepreneurs who had made millions of dollars, who lost it all and never made it back. And the reason why is because they focus just on buying ads or just on ranking on SEO and as soon as those things shift, the algorithms change and, uh, you know, um, something happens, they lose their business overnight. Okay. I'm lucky that they were my first mentors, Mark Joyner told me from day number one, he said, the only thing you should be focusing on growing your company is building your list, building your list, building your list, building your list and you drill that in my head. And so everything we do when we drive traffic, all has one goal and one goal only is to take all the traffic from the, for our dream 100 to the traffic we earn and the traffic we control and pushing it into our own list. So we control our destiny. Okay. Marked my word, there will be a time in the near future. I don't know if it's going to be a week from now, a month from now, a year from now, five years from now, where Facebook ads are not going to work for the majority of us. It's going to happen. So right now we've got the season, right? Instagram, YouTube, these things are not always going to be around. And so if you're focusing everything, like just making sales off these platforms, this is good for a season, but it's not going to keep you here 15 years from now. Okay. You do that by focusing on building a list and building a relationship with the list. Okay. So all the efforts we're doing to drive traffic, all those things are all with one goal, one goal only is to build your list. And that's the traffic you control traffic you own. Okay. If Zuckerberg shut me down tomorrow, I'd be fine. Okay. If Larry and Sergey wanted to de-index everything we got, we're going to be okay because we've got our customer lists. Okay. And that's the focus point. So that is the gist of the first two sections of the traffic secrets book.
